summary: Follow-up discovered while introducing the smallest healthy authoritative state machine for release closeout.

observations.surface: ops/scripts/release_evidence_dashboard.py, ops/scripts/learning_readiness_signoff_revalidation.py, Makefile clean/conditional release targets, README.md, ops/README.md
observations.problem: The closeout report introduced authoritative machine/operator release decision fields before downstream consumers had switched over, so dashboard and signoff revalidation could still misrepresent conditional or unknown closeout states.
observations.why_it_matters: Closeout is only a reliable source of truth when nearby automation reads the authoritative decision model instead of reconstructing policy from legacy booleans or checked-in component status.
observations.automation_candidate: Move the nearest closeout consumers to release_readiness_state, machine_release_allowed, operator_release_allowed, and requires_accepted_risk_review, then remove the obsolete release_ready field from the closeout contract.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated for release_evidence_dashboard, learning_readiness_signoff_revalidation, explicit check-clean/check-conditional and release-clean/release-conditional Make targets, report-contract-closeout release-smoke reuse/currentness precheck, generated source-contract fingerprint exclusions, tests, docs, and checked-in canonical artifacts. No duplicate follow-up artifact is needed for the clean/conditional lane split.
